prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline (C₆H₅NH₂, 14 atoms: 6C + 1N + 7H) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme in PySCF using a finite-difference approach: neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, multiplicity 2) single-point calculations were performed. All SCFs converged. Fukui charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No warnings were emitted.

Headline result — Most electrophilic atom: C (atom 3, ortho carbon), f⁺ = 2.2975867. Most nucleophilic atom: N (atom 1, amino nitrogen), f⁻ = 0.20452359. The ortho/para ring carbons (C3, C4, C7) show the highest electrophilic Fukui values, consistent with the well-known ortho/para-directing activating character of the −NH₂ group in electrophilic aromatic substitution.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/.../aniline.xyz. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-172445_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
